title: Horridocactus echinus
taxonomy: Horridocactus echinus is classified within the family of Cactaceae, which is a large family of flowering plants that includes over 1,500 species of cacti. It belongs to the subfamily of Cactoideae and the tribe of Cacteae. The genus Horridocactus is a part of the subfamily Pereskioideae, but recent studies have moved it to Cactoideae. The species echinus is part of the Horridocactus genus. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant within its family.

intro: Horridocactus echinus, also known as the hedgehog prickly pear, is a species of cactus that belongs to the genus Horridocactus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, providing relief from pain, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Horridocactus echinus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems.
